 {"id":"bd-1006","title":"Implement cmd_self_update()","description":"**Implement self-update subcommand**

## What
Update ru itself to the latest version from GitHub releases.

## Why
Users shouldn't have to re-run the installer to update.

## Options
- --check: Check for updates without installing

## Flow
1. Get current version from $VERSION
2. Fetch latest release tag from GitHub API
3. Compare versions
4. If newer, download and verify checksum
5. Replace current script

## Implementation Notes
- Use gh api for authenticated requests
- Verify SHA256 checksum
- Atomic replacement (write to temp, then mv)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checks for updates
- Downloads securely with checksum
- Atomic update (no partial writes)
- --check only reports, doesn't update","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["commands"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1006","depends_on_id":"bd-10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.965865062-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1006","depends_on_id":"bd-202","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.995827747-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1007","title":"Implement process_single_repo()","description":"**Orchestrate processing of a single repository**

## What
Function that handles all logic for one repo: check status, decide action, execute.

## Why
Centralizes the per-repo logic that sync and status share.

## Flow
```
1. Parse repo spec (URL, branch, custom name)
2. Resolve local path
3. Check if path exists
4. If exists:
   a. Verify it's a git repo
   b. Check remote URL matches
   c. Get status (behind/ahead/dirty)
   d. Execute action (pull/skip)
5. If not exists:
   a. Clone repo
6. Record result
```

## Implementation
```bash
process_single_repo() {
    local spec="$1"
    local action="$2"  # sync|status
    
    local url branch local_name
    parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
    
    local path
    if [[ -n "$local_name" ]]; then
        path="$PROJECTS_DIR/$local_name"
    else
        path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
    fi
    
    local repo_name
    repo_name=$(basename "$path")
    
    log_step "Processing: $repo_name"
    
    if [[ -d "$path" ]]; then
        if ! is_git_repo "$path"; then
            log_warn "Not a git repo: $path"
            write_result "$repo_name" "skip" "not_git" "" ""
            return 0
        fi
        
        if ! check_remote_mismatch "$path" "$url"; then
            log_warn "Remote mismatch, skipping: $repo_name"
            write_result "$repo_name" "skip" "remote_mismatch" "" ""
            return 0
        fi
        
        if [[ "$action" == "sync" ]]; then
            do_pull "$path" "$repo_name" "$UPDATE_STRATEGY"
        else
            # status only
            local status
            status=$(get_repo_status "$path" "true")
            # Report status...
        fi
    else
        if [[ "$action" == "sync" ]]; then
            do_clone "$url" "$path" "$repo_name"
        else
            log_info "Not cloned: $repo_name"
            write_result "$repo_name" "status" "missing" "" ""
        fi
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Handles all cases
- Records all results
- Doesn't stop on errors","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:39:42.411500571-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:39:42.411500571-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script","labels":["commands"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1007","depends_on_id":"bd-801","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.025226921-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1007","depends_on_id":"bd-804","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.054912164-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1007","depends_on_id":"bd-806","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.116140631-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1007","depends_on_id":"bd-807","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.150654873-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1008","title":"Implement main processing loop","description":"**Main loop with progress display**

## What
The loop that iterates through all repos with progress indication.

## Why
Users need to see progress during long operations.

## Progress Display
```
→ Processing 12/47: coding_agent_session_search
  ├─ Path: /data/projects/coding_agent_session_search
  ├─ Status: behind (0 ahead, 3 behind)
  ├─ Action: git pull --ff-only
  └─ Result: Updated (2s)
```

## Implementation
```bash
run_processing_loop() {
    local action="$1"  # sync|status
    local repos
    repos=$(get_all_repos)
    
    if [[ -z "$repos" ]]; then
        log_info "No repositories to process."
        return 0
    fi
    
    local total
    total=$(echo "$repos" | wc -l)
    local current=0
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        ((current++))
        log_step "Processing $current/$total: $(basename "$spec")"
        process_single_repo "$spec" "$action"
    done <<< "$repos"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shows progress (N/M)
- Processes all repos
- Continues on errors
- Works with empty list","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:39:42.412822782-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:39:42.412822782-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script","labels":["commands"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1008","depends_on_id":"bd-1007","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.181635576-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1008","depends_on_id":"bd-905","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.212359917-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1009","title":"Implement cmd_remove()","description":"**Implement remove subcommand - remove repo from list**

## What
The `ru remove` command to remove a repository from the list.

## Why
Users need to remove repos without manually editing list files. This is a fundamental CRUD operation that was missing from the original plan.

## Usage
```bash
ru remove owner/repo
ru remove owner/repo --all  # Remove from all lists
ru remove --from-cwd        # Remove current directory's repo
```

## Implementation
```bash
cmd_remove() {
    local repo="$1"
    local list_file="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/repos.d/repos.txt"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$list_file" ]]; then
        log_error "No repos configured"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Find and remove matching line
    local pattern
    pattern=$(echo "$repo" | sed 's/[.[\/^$*]/\\&/g')
    
    if grep -q "$pattern" "$list_file"; then
        sed -i "/$pattern/d" "$list_file"
        log_success "Removed: $repo"
    else
        log_warn "Not found in list: $repo"
        return 1
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Removes repo from list
- Handles 'not found' gracefully
- Works with partial matches (owner/repo matches full URL)
- --from-cwd extracts from git remote","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["commands"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1009","depends_on_id":"bd-10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.403168479-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1009","depends_on_id":"bd-901","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.433158697-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-101","title":"Create VERSION file","description":"**Create VERSION file with initial version 1.0.0**

## What
Create a VERSION file at the repository root containing the semver version string.

## Why
Single source of truth for version. The script reads this file, CI validates consistency, and releases are tagged from it. No version strings embedded in multiple places.

## Implementation
```bash
echo '1.0.0' > VERSION
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- VERSION file exists at repo root
- Contains exactly '1.0.0' (no trailing newline issues)
- Is readable by shell: `cat VERSION`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:30.470606513-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:30.470606513-05:00","close_reason":"VERSION file already exists (1.0.0)","labels":["setup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-101","depends_on_id":"bd-1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.744605613-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1010","title":"Implement cmd_prune()","description":"**Implement prune subcommand - find orphan repos**

## What
The `ru prune` command to identify and optionally archive repos in PROJECTS_DIR that aren't in any list.

## Why
Over time, users clone repos manually or remove entries from lists. These 'orphan' repos waste disk space and cause confusion. The prune command helps identify them.

## Usage
```bash
ru prune              # List orphans (dry run)
ru prune --archive    # Move orphans to ~/.local/state/ru/archived/
ru prune --delete     # Actually delete (requires confirmation)
```

## Implementation
```bash
cmd_prune() {
    local action="${1:-list}"
    local configured_paths
    configured_paths=$(get_all_repos | while read spec; do
        url_to_local_path "$spec" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT"
    done | sort -u)
    
    # Find all git repos in PROJECTS_DIR
    local actual_repos
    actual_repos=$(find "$PROJECTS_DIR" -maxdepth 2 -name .git -type d | \
        xargs -I{} dirname {} | sort -u)
    
    # Find orphans (in actual but not in configured)
    local orphans
    orphans=$(comm -23 <(echo "$actual_repos") <(echo "$configured_paths"))
    
    if [[ -z "$orphans" ]]; then
        log_info "No orphan repositories found"
        return 0
    fi
    
    echo "$orphans" | while read path; do
        log_warn "Orphan: $path"
    done
    
    # Handle archive/delete based on action
}
```

## Safety
- Default is LIST ONLY (dry run)
- Archive moves to dated subfolder, never deletes
- Delete requires explicit --delete AND confirmation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Identifies repos not in any list
- Safe default (list only)
- Archive preserves repos safely
- Never deletes without explicit confirmation","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["commands"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1010","depends_on_id":"bd-10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.465248731-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1010","depends_on_id":"bd-905","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.496361533-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1010","depends_on_id":"bd-703","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.527218564-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1011","title":"Implement parallel sync with worker pool","description":"**Implement parallel sync with worker pool**

## What
Add `--parallel N` (or `-j N`) option to sync N repos concurrently.

## Why
Syncing 50+ repos sequentially takes minutes. With parallelism, we can saturate the network and reduce total time dramatically.

## Design (from plan section 15.1)
- Worker pool pattern with configurable worker count
- Default: `PARALLEL=1` (sequential, current behavior)
- Option: `--parallel N` or `-j N` (N workers)
- Each worker writes to shared NDJSON results file (atomic appends)
- Progress: single progress bar showing completed/total instead of per-repo spinners
- Rate limiting awareness: if we detect 429s, auto-reduce parallelism

## Implementation Sketch
```bash
PARALLEL=${PARALLEL:-1}
if [[ "$PARALLEL" -gt 1 ]]; then
    # Create a work queue (one line per repo)
    printf '%s\n' "${repos[@]}" > "$WORK_QUEUE"
    
    # Launch N workers
    for ((i=0; i<PARALLEL; i++)); do
        (
            while IFS= read -r repo; do
                process_single_repo "$repo"
            done < <(flock -x 200; head -1 "$WORK_QUEUE"; sed -i '1d' "$WORK_QUEUE")
        ) 200>"$LOCK_FILE" &
        worker_pids+=($!)
    done
    
    # Wait for all workers
    wait "${worker_pids[@]}"
fi
```

## Progress Display
```
Syncing repositories [=====>          ] 23/47 (49%)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- `--parallel 4` processes 4 repos concurrently
- NDJSON results are correctly aggregated
- Progress bar shows overall completion
- Works correctly when some repos fail
- No race conditions in result file writes
- Config file can set default PARALLEL value","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["performance","v1-stretch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1011","depends_on_id":"bd-10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:14.01343846-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1011","depends_on_id":"bd-1001","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:14.043409351-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1011","depends_on_id":"bd-1105","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:14.073618481-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
